name: Diffract CLI Commands
description: Diffract is our branded UI/CLI layer on top of NemoClaw. 'diffract' is a symlink to 'nemoclaw' — all commands identical. Setup: diffract onboard, diffract list, diffract status. Sandbox: diffract <name> connect/status/logs/policy-add/policy-list/destroy. Credentials: diffract credentials list/reset <KEY>. Services: diffract start/stop. Other: diffract debug, diffract uninstall, diffract --version. Web UI project is in nemoclaw-ui/ (Next.js, package name 'diffract').

title: Diffract is branded layer over NemoClaw + OpenShell
rationale: Diffract is our product name. CLI is a symlink (diffract -> nemoclaw). Web UI (Next.js) in nemoclaw-ui/ replaces interactive CLI with single-form deploy + dashboard. Underlying NemoClaw env vars (NEMOCLAW_*) preserved since that's what the CLI reads internally. Enterprise target: SSO, RBAC, multi-tenant planned.

name: chat-ws-loopback-reorigination-hop
description: Chat /chat WebSocket on the dashboard (port 9119) requires a LOOPBACK client peer (web_server.py _ws_client_is_allowed, a DNS-rebinding defence). Behind Caddy a plain host socat forwards from the docker BRIDGE IP -> non-loopback peer -> WS upgrade 403 -> chat dead. An openshell/ssh -L tunnel does NOT fix it: the dashboard is docker-exec'd into the CONTAINER netns while the openshell ssh session lands in the sandbox WORKLOAD netns -> tunnel binds but 502s (no listener there). FIX (transport-only, no rebuild): in-container socat hop re-originates from the container's own 127.0.0.1 so the dashboard sees a loopback peer. Chain host 127.0.0.1:9119 -> CONTAINER_IP:9118 (bridge) -> 127.0.0.1:9119 (in container). Host listener bound to 127.0.0.1 (Caddy dials loopback). Lives in setup.sh sandbox-port-forwarder.sh with an end-to-end curl keepalive. Caveat: behind a reverse proxy the peer is always the proxy, so this neutralises the peer check in effect; real gates are the per-session token + Origin check. Verified 403->101 through Caddy + survives cold systemctl restart (commit ee41c4b).

Dashboard /chat shows 'Chat unavailable: the ptyprocess package is missing' even though the WS handshake is 101. The embedded TUI chat (--tui) spawns a PTY via hermes_cli/pty_bridge.py needing ptyprocess; PtyBridge.spawn() raises PtyUnavailableError and web_server.py:3517 sends the error as a TEXT FRAME over an already-established socket -> WS 101 does NOT prove chat works (never discriminated this bug). Root cause: NemoClaw/agents/hermes/Dockerfile.base HERMES_UV_EXTRAS defaulted to 'messaging web' (no pty), so uv sync --frozen never installed the [pty] extra (uv.lock pins ptyprocess==0.7.0 under extra==pty). CHAT endpoint is /api/pty (NOT /api/ws).
resolution: (1) Dockerfile.base HERMES_UV_EXTRAS='messaging web pty' (root cause, baked on base rebuild, covers fresh client deploys); (2) setup.sh sandbox-port-forwarder.sh presence-checked idempotent 'pip install ptyprocess==0.7.0' before the --tui launch (self-heals containers recreated by redeploys without a rebuild; no-op once image carries pty). Verify by reading the post-101 frame on /api/pty: working chat streams terminal output (Diffract TUI banner), broken sends 'Chat unavailable'. Commit 41909ca.

name: dashboard-hermes-rebrand-safe-sweep
description: Removing visible Hermes branding from the dashboards. SAFE RULE: replace the standalone capital-H brand word via perl s/\bHermes\b/Diffract/g ONLY on pure-UI-text files: hermes/web/index.html (the title tag), hermes/web/src/i18n/*.ts (16 locales; the word boundary spares camelCase KEYS like updateHermes/updatingHermes so only string VALUES change), hermes/web/src/themes/presets.ts (Hermes Teal). Plus targeted edits for the session-token error strings in hermes/web/src/lib/api.ts and gatewayClient.ts, and diffractui Dashboard.tsx (Hermes Web Dashboard). MUST NOT touch (a blanket sed corrupts these contracts): the X-Hermes-Session-Token HTTP header (api.ts:31), window.__HERMES_* globals, HERMES_BASE_PATH, the /api/hermes/ routes, the HERMES_QWEN_ env prefix. Left truthful because they are real things (do not fake): the nemohermes CLI command (diffractui Dashboard.tsx:301), the ~/.hermes config paths plus hermes CLI command refs shown in the Env/Config/Plugins/Skills screens, and code/CSS comments. tsc and npm run build catch any accidental key rename. DEPLOY CHAINS: agent dashboard (hermes/web) builds to hermes_cli/web_dist; the LIVE source of truth is the HOST agent dir /usr/local/lib/hermes-agent (the sandbox-port-forwarder docker-cps its web_dist into the container on every restart), NOT the repo - so rebuild THERE then docker cp into the container /opt/hermes/web_dist. diffractui runs next start from the repo at /root/diffract-main/diffractui - git pull, npm run build (.next), systemctl restart diffractui. GOTCHA fixed: setup.sh _overlay_host copied web/src but not web/index.html, which is why the browser title survived the earlier rebrand. Verify by grepping the BUILT bundle / .next AND the live served /agent/ title, not just that it built. Commits 12a1cf9 (source) and the live redeploy.

title: Genericize ~/.hermes paths/command refs in dashboard UI (not fake, not leave)
rationale: User wanted NO visible hermes in the dashboard but the ~/.hermes config paths and hermes/nemohermes command refs are real. Faking them as ~/.diffract would show paths that do not exist on the box; leaving them keeps hermes visible. Chosen middle path: genericize - strip the ~/.hermes/ prefix so paths read config.yaml / plugins/ / skills/ (truthful, just relative), remove the parenthetical CLI-parity notes, and reword English (Remove this plugin?, No skills found.). Applied across all 17 locales incl full-width Chinese parens + a Hungarian declension. Left ONLY invisible contracts (X-Hermes-Session-Token, __HERMES_* globals, /api/hermes routes, HERMES_QWEN_, hermes_home field), the nemohermes CLI command, and code comments. STILL OPEN: HERMES_DOCS_URL=https://hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/docs/ (the Docs tab) is an external site, not a label - flagged to user. Commits 12a1cf9 + b38d9c5. See [[dashboard-hermes-rebrand-safe-sweep]].

name: agent-self-identity-soul-md
description: The chat agents self-identity (what it says when greeting, e.g. I am Hermes Agent running in the NemoClaw sandbox) is driven by SOUL.md at HERMES_HOME (= /sandbox/.hermes/SOUL.md in the container). SOUL.md is slot #1 of the system prompt (agent/system_prompt.py via load_soul_md in agent/prompt_builder.py) and OVERRIDES DEFAULT_AGENT_IDENTITY. The deployment SOUL.md is baked by NemoClaw/agents/hermes/Dockerfile (was an inline printf with Hermes/NemoClaw/OpenShell text). A SECOND, code-level source of the word Hermes Agent is HERMES_AGENT_HELP_GUIDANCE (prompt_builder.py), always appended in system_prompt.py. KEY FINDING (verified with hermes -z one-shot): a strong positive slot-#1 SOUL.md dominates BOTH the help-guidance Hermes mention AND the models inference of NemoClaw/OpenShell from env vars (NEMOCLAW_*, OPENSHELL_*) - so you do NOT need to edit upstream agent Python. FIX: edit SOUL.md only (data, safe to apply live; NEVER live-patch the agent Python/TUI - version skew ends the session). Use a positive identity (advisor: do not enumerate forbidden brands - that primes them) plus a generic do-not-describe-the-platform line. Single source of truth: NemoClaw/agents/hermes/SOUL.md, COPYd by the Dockerfile (fresh deploys) and re-applied live by setup.sh (host stage + self-healing forwarder step that overwrites an old-marker SOUL on each container start, idempotent/self-deactivating). VERIFY with hermes -z greeting + an adversarial who/where probe, not by cat-ing the file. Commit 1d2af4d. Related [[dashboard-hermes-rebrand-safe-sweep]].

name: dashboard-chat-via-gateway-v1-api
description: The agent dashboard /agent Chat tab can be a normal message-bubble chat (not the xterm TUI). The dashboard backend (web_server.py) only exposes /api/pty (the terminal), but the agent also runs an OpenAI-compatible gateway on :8642 that Caddy proxies at origin /v1/* (handle /v1/* reverse_proxy 127.0.0.1:8642). That gateway runs the FULL agent (tools/skills/SOUL; ~14.5k prompt tokens) with NO auth (API_SERVER_KEY unset, /v1/models returns 200) and standard OpenAI SSE streaming (data: {choices:[{delta:{content}}]}), model id hermes-agent. So a chat UI is FRONTEND-ONLY: rewrite hermes/web/src/pages/ChatPage.tsx to POST /v1/chat/completions (leading slash = origin root, NOT under the /agent base path) with stream:true and render bubbles; keep the { isActive } prop so App.tsx persistent chat host is unchanged. Deploy like any web change (rebuild web_dist on the host agent dir + docker cp into the container) - no agent image rebuild. The Chat tab visibility is gated by __HERMES_DASHBOARD_EMBEDDED_CHAT__ (set true by hermes dashboard --tui), which stays true. Removing the TUI also removes its Hermes Agent welcome banner. Commit on this work.

The chat UI backend (agent OpenAI-compatible gateway, Caddy /v1/* -> 127.0.0.1:8642) returns 502 on fresh sandboxes because the gateway runs in the sandbox WORKLOAD netns, not the container bridge netns. The gateway is started by nemoclaw-start over the openshell ssh session; its api_server listens on 127.0.0.1:18642 in the workload netns, fronted by an in-sandbox socat on :8642 (also workload netns). docker exec lands in the CONTAINER (bridge) netns and CANNOT see 8642/18642 (ss shows nothing, curl 000) - that is the diagnostic tell. setup.sh port-forwarder used socat TCP-LISTEN:8642 -> CONTAINER_IP:8642 (bridge), which cannot reach the workload netns -> 502. It worked only when onboards own openshell forward won the :8642 bind race over the socat (flaky). This is the SAME netns split as the 9119 chat-WS issue, opposite direction (9119 dashboard = container netns -> in-container loopback hop; 8642 gateway = workload netns -> ssh -L tunnel).
resolution: Replace the 8642 socat in setup.sh sandbox-port-forwarder.sh with an openshell loopback forward (openshell forward start --background 8642 SANDBOX, an ssh -L that lands in the workload netns) + a keepalive (re-establish if the 127.0.0.1:8642 listener drops). Live unblock: kill the 8642 socat, openshell forward start --background 8642 hermes, then curl 127.0.0.1:8642/health = 200. Verified durable across a forwarder restart. Commit on this work.

description: After fixing the 8642 502, the chat UI still failed with 403. Cause: the agent gateways api_server rejects requests that carry a browser Origin (and/or Referer) header as a DNS-rebinding/CSRF defence, and its allowed-origins list does not include the public host. Diagnostic tell: server-side curl to /v1/chat/completions with NO Origin returns 200, but the same request WITH -H Origin: https://<host> returns 403 (response header server: aiohttp = the gateway, not Caddy). Browsers always send Origin on JSON POSTs, so the same-origin chat UI is always 403d.
resolution: Strip Origin (and Referer) on Caddys /v1/* reverse_proxy: add header_up -Origin and header_up -Referer inside the handle /v1/* reverse_proxy block (both the IP and domain CADDY_CONFIG variants in setup.sh). Caddy is a trusted proxy fronting the gateway; same-origin is the only legit client; cross-origin reads stay blocked by the browser CORS (gateway sends no ACAO). Live: edit /etc/caddy/Caddyfile + systemctl reload caddy; verify POST /v1 with an Origin header is 200. SECURITY NOTE: /v1 is unauthenticated, so this removes the browser-origin guard for it - fine for the same-origin dashboard behind the admin gate, revisit if /v1 is exposed more broadly. Related [[gateway-8642-workload-netns-socat-502]] and [[dashboard-chat-via-gateway-v1-api]].

title: Persist dashboard chat conversations in localStorage, not server sessions
rationale: User wanted chat history to survive reload and to revisit old conversations. The agent gateway /v1/chat/completions is STATELESS (no session id returned to continue a conversation), and the agents own sessions (/api/sessions, source=api_server) live inside the sandbox and are DESTROYED whenever the sandbox is recreated - which this user does frequently. So server sessions cannot reliably back the chat history. Chose client-side localStorage (key diffract.chat.v1: conversations[] + activeId) in hermes/web ChatPage.tsx: a conversation sidebar (new/select/delete) + persist on every change. Survives reloads AND sandbox recreations (browser-side). History is resent to /v1 each turn (OpenAI-style). Tradeoffs: per-browser (not cross-device), and continuing an old chat after a recreate is answered from the visible transcript only (fresh agent, no server memory). If cross-device/server-backed history is needed later, use the OpenAI Responses API (/v1/responses exists, stateful) or /api/sessions, accepting they vanish on sandbox destroy. Frontend-only, no backend/image change. See [[dashboard-chat-via-gateway-v1-api]].
